steps.title: Spot the main verb
steps.content: The sentence ends with “analyzed geotagged social media posts,” which is the primary action the researchers performed. This identifies “analyzed” as the main verb of the sentence.

steps.title: Choose a participle, not a second main verb
steps.content: The blank cannot be another main verb, because “analyzed” is already the main verb and there is no coordinating conjunction joining them. “Looking” is a participle (a non-finite verb form) that modifies “researchers” and allows “analyzed” to be the sole main verb. “Were looking,” “looked,” and “had looked” are all finite verb forms that would create two main verbs without coordination, which violates sentence structure. This matches choice C — C is correct.
